title: polypodium ellipticum var. furcans
intro: Polypodium ellipticum var. furcans, also known as the forked polypody, is a type of fern that belongs to the Polypodiaceae family and is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The forked polypody has been used to treat various health problems, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. It can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
